question:
These young men, who are often ill-equipped themselves and poorly paid, work daily in dangerous conditions to help climbers achieve their goals. Vern's response was to found Kili Summit Club.

Why did Vern found Kili Summit Club?
choices: To train tourists to climb Mount Kilimanjaro safely
To help the ill-equipped and poorly paid guides
To raise money for his own mountain adventures
To attract more tourists to the Serengeti

question: In the text 'Getting Your Message Across', the underlined pronoun 'they' in 'We asked three people to tell us what they think about the different ways people make themselves heard' refers to:

As far as I'm concerned, social media is the obvious way to get your message across. On a more personal level, I let my friends know my views on social networks and I would support a campaign to protect animal rights.

What campaign would the author support?

She even did a 24-hour fund-raising activity, bringing in $26,593. This is a:

a) independent clause b) dependent clause. Which is correct?
choices: It is an independent clause because it makes sense on its own.
It is a dependent clause because it doesn't make sense on its own.
It is both independent and dependent.
It cannot be classified as either.

question: According to the Active Reading box, 'a clause which makes sense on its own' is called:
The word 'protect against' means:
choices: take care of someone or something

question: The word 'participate in' means:
choices: take care of someone or something
take action to solve a problem

question: The word 'deal with' means:
choices: stay alive by using the object
take action to solve a problem

question: The word 'call out to' means:
choices: take action to solve a problem

question: The word 'survive on' means:
choices: stay alive by using the object of the sentence

question: When writing an article, if you want to make the introduction engaging, you could:
choices: give a personal opinion in the conclusion
give a surprising fact or statistic
repeat the words from the title

question: When writing an article conclusion, which of the following is a good strategy?
choices: use a pun in the title
address your readers directly for the first time

question: The title 'Happy hyenas help people of Harar' uses which writing technique?
The title 'Food for thought' uses which writing technique?
The title 'Can we like the hyena?' uses which writing technique?

'We didn't have our keys with us.'
Tariq told Nabil that ________.
choices: they hadn't had their keys with them.
they didn't have their keys with them.
they haven't had their keys with us.
they hadn't have their keys with them.

question:
'My parents spent every day of their lives together.'
Sara said that ________.
choices: her parents spent every day of their lives together.
her parents had spent every day of their lives together.
her parents has spent every day of their lives together.
her parents were spending every day of their lives together.

question:
'Rasha doesn't want to see you tonight.'
She told him that ________.
choices: Rasha doesn't want to see him tonight.
Rasha didn't want to see him that night.
Rasha hadn't wanted to see him that night.
Rasha won't want to see him tonight.

question:
'I am going to London this summer.'
My brother said ________.
choices: he is going to London this summer.
he was going to London that summer.
he had gone to London this summer.
he would go to London this summer.

question:
'I've found you a new phone.'
He told me (that) ________.
choices: he has found me a new phone.
he had found me a new phone.
he found me a new phone.
he finds me a new phone.

question: We do NOT change direct speech into reported speech when the reporting verb is in the ________ tense.
We may choose NOT to change direct speech when the action is still happening or is going to happen. Which example shows this?
choices: 'He says it's really important to help all the villages in the area.'
'He said he's going to do the same at another village next week as well.'
'He says he's really determined to make the project work.'

question: We may also choose NOT to change when the direct speech describes a general truth or fact. Which example shows this?
choices: 'He said he's going to do the same at another village next week as well.'
'He says it's really important to help all the villages in the area.'
'He says he's really determined to make the project work.'

question:
The professor said that dirty water had mixed with clean water and that had made people ill.

What did the professor say originally in direct speech?
choices: 'Dirty water will mix with clean water and that will make people ill.'
'Dirty water mixed with clean water and this made people ill.'
'Dirty water has mixed with clean water and that has made people ill.'
'Dirty water was mixing with clean water and that was making people ill.'

question: Mazen agreed ________ the old man was discriminated against.
Nader offered ________ lend Maher his car for the weekend.
The man at the information desk advised ________ to leave our phone number.

'I will cook for you on Saturday.' (Nadia is speaking)
Nadia ________.
choices: promised to cooked for me on Saturday.
promised cook for me on Saturday.
promised to cook for me on Saturday.
promised to cook for you on Saturday.

question: The pronunciation rule for aspiration states that we add a puff of air after /p/, /t/ and /k/ ONLY if the sound is at the beginning of a stressed syllable AND:
choices: the sound is at the end of a word
the sound is immediately followed by a vowel
the sound is followed by another consonant
the sound is in a two-syllable word

question: The correct pronunciation of the underlined letter in the word 'paper' is:
In the word pairs for pronunciation, 'equality' and 'equalise' have ________ word stress.
In the word pairs for pronunciation, 'institution' and 'institutionalise' have ________ word stress.
Students went to a meeting about climate change yesterday. Our reporter asked student representative, Alia Altahhan why ________ joined the meeting.
Alia asked why ________ doing more about cutting pollution.
The employees said: 'No, we won't work late.' The employees ________ to work late.

'I hear you've graduated, Samia. Congratulations!' (Nour is speaking)
The reporting verb 'congratulate' follows the pattern:
The verb 'apologise for' follows the pattern:
The verb 'insist on' follows the pattern:

Read this passage and answer the question:

As a teenager, the sight of Mount Kilimanjaro rising majestically above the Serengeti made Vern Jones desperately want to climb it. Finally in 2014, at the age of 62, Vern finally realised his dream while accompanied by his daughters and their husbands.

At what age did Vern Jones finally climb Mount Kilimanjaro?

Read this passage and answer the question:

Hyenas are big, ugly and dangerous animals and they are the most common large predator in Africa. But in the old walled city of Harar, the hyenas are no longer a danger to people.

What is the most common large predator in Africa?
